content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES Coi’IrussIoN
P.O. Box 807
• 428 J Street
Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660
• Fax (916) 322-0886
November 12, 1998
Dario L. Marenco
Supervisor, Second District
San Joaquin County Board of Supervisors
222 East Weber Avenue
Room 701, Courthouse
Stockton, California 95202
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. 1-98-254
Dear Mr. Marenco:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the “Act”).’ Since your request is one for general guidance, we are treating
your request as one for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c).
2
Please keep in mind that the Commission does not act as a finder of fact when issuing
advice. (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71.) Our advice is applicable only to the extent that
the facts provided to us are correct, and that all of the material facts have been provided. Our
advice is limited to the provisions of the Act. We cannot provide you with advice regarding any
other laws that may be applicable to you.
QUESTION
As a current member of the Board of Supervisors, may you participate in election
campaigns for candidates in the June 2000, election?
Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109
-
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal
written advice. (Section 83114; and Regulation 18329(c)(3).)


File No. 1-98-254
Page 2
CONCLUSION
Under the provisions of the Political Reform Act, your position as a member of the Board
of Supervisors does not preclude you from participating in election campaigns for other
candidates.
FACTS
You are a current member of the San Joaquin County Board of Supervisors. In June of
2000, there will be an election for three seats on the Board of Supervisors. Two candidates for
those seats have asked for your help with their campaigns.
ANALYSIS
The Political Reform Act does not prohibit a candidate or elected official from
participating in the election campaign of another candidate or elected official. However, when a
candidate or elected official participates in the election campaign of another candidate or elected
official, he or she should be aware of certain provisions of the Act that may be applicable.
One such provision is the prohibition against transferring campaign funds from one
campaign to another, found in Section 85306. This provision was enacted in 1996 as part of
Proposition 208. Enforcement of this provision is currently enjoined by order of the United
States District Court for the Eastern District of California, but the case is now being reviewed on
appeal.
3 If the injunction is lifted, and the section is held to be valid, it would prohibit you from
contributing any of your campaign funds to the other candidates.
Another such provision is Regulation 18215, defining what constitutes a contribution. If
you or your campaign makes a payment at the behest of another candidate to publicize your
endorsement of that candidate, the payment is a contribution to the other candidate’s campaign.
Under subsection (c)(6) of the regulation, however, if the other candidate’s campaign makes the
payment to publicize your endorsement of the candidate, that payment is not a contribution from
you or your campaign.
In addition to these provisions of the Act, there may be other provisions of the Act that
would be especially applicable to your situation, depending upon the specific kind of help that
you intend to provide to the other campaigns. In the future, you may want to ask us for advice
regarding your specific plans to help the other campaigns, when those plans become known to
you.
3 California Prolife Council PohticalAction Committee v. Jan Scully, et al. (1998) 989 F.Supp. 1282.


File No. 1-98-254
Page 3
Finally, you should be aware of any local ordinances that may apply to your situation.
As we can only give you advice regarding provisions of the Act, you should consult your county
counsel for guidance concerning any applicable local ordinances.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at
(916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel
By:
Steven Benito Russo
Staff Counsel, Legal Division
